scopeNote: Targeted milestone validators passed locally after `MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn spotless:apply` cleared branch-local formatting drift. Four scrutiny reviews were produced for the completed milestone features, and all four failed with blocking correctness regressions in shared dealer-master preservation, dealer receipt replay/allocation integrity, dealer auto-settle implicit identity, and supplier auto-settle ordering/reference behavior.

lint.command: cd erp-domain && MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn spotless:apply && MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn spotless:check

blockingIssues.description: `DealerService.createDealer` still forces the surviving shared dealer master to `ACTIVE`, so re-running sales onboarding clears preserved `ON_HOLD` / `SUSPENDED` / `BLOCKED` state instead of preserving it.

`AdminUserService.createDealerForUser` also forces matched dealers to `ACTIVE`, so tenant-admin role assignment reactivates held or suspended dealers and breaks the required shared-master preservation contract.

Dealer receipt replay still returns success when the journal/payment event exists but explicit allocation rows are missing, so the payment-event-first corridor can persist journal-only truth without the canonical applied/unapplied allocation rows.

Dealer auto-settle now derives fallback identity before allocations are resolved, so repeated same-amount calls without explicit `referenceNumber` or `idempotencyKey` can replay the first posting instead of settling the next oldest invoice.

Supplier auto-settle now generates fallback reference/idempotency values without the resolved allocation set, so keyless same-amount requests can collide across different open-purchase sets and replay the first posting.

The feature claims `VAL-PARTNER-016`, but supplier auto-settle still orders only by `invoiceDate` then `id`; the contract requires due date when available, then invoice date, then ID, and the current purchasing model does not yet express that due-date-first rule.

appliedUpdates.description: Added four scrutiny review artifacts for the completed party-master-and-payment-core implementation features, covering dealer shared-master preservation, dealer receipt payment-event replay, dealer settlement auto-settle identity, and supplier settlement ordering/reference behavior.
Recorded the validator outcomes and synthesized the blocking scrutiny findings for this milestone so the orchestrator can queue repair packets before user testing.
Ran `MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn spotless:apply` to clear branch-local Spotless drift so the milestone lint validator could be rechecked successfully.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For shared dealer-master packets, require explicit proof that re-onboarding an already held/suspended dealer through both the sales dealer path and the tenant-admin `ROLE_DEALER` assignment path preserves the existing dealer status instead of reactivating it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed review for `shared-dealer-master-preservation-hardcut` found both `DealerService.createDealer` and `AdminUserService.createDealerForUser` still force matched dealers to `ACTIVE`, and the packet's validation only proved happy-path convergence rather than re-onboarding preserved held/suspended dealers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For payment-event and settlement packets, require focused regression proof for the keyless fallback identity path (no explicit `referenceNumber` / `idempotencyKey`) and for replay against degraded journal/payment-event states where canonical allocation rows are missing.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed reviews for `dealer-payment-event-receipt-hardcut` and `dealer-settlement-and-allocation-hardcut` both found correctness holes that only appear when replay/key generation relies on implicit fallback behavior rather than the explicit-key happy path covered in the new tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile `VAL-PARTNER-016` with the current purchasing model by either adding a due-date field and due-date-first ordering support to supplier auto-settle or narrowing the contract to the strongest ordering rule the codebase can actually express today.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed review for `supplier-payment-and-ap-settlement-hardcut` found `RawMaterialPurchase` has no due-date field and `RawMaterialPurchaseRepository` still orders supplier settlement candidates by `invoiceDate, id`, while the validation contract presently requires due date when available, then invoice date, then ID.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update `.factory/library/user-testing.md` immediately with a freshly provisioned dealer-user password-change / MailHog note.
rejectedObservations.reason: Not applied in this scrutiny packet because this worker run is constrained from editing repository documentation unless explicitly requested; the factual gap is preserved here for orchestrator triage.

rejectedObservations.observation: Broaden shared-state guidance right now to document every packet-specific replay edge case discovered in these four reviews.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected for this round because the observations are accurate but still feature-specific; I only elevated the systemic test-gap patterns above and left narrower packet details in the review artifacts.
